title: Realtime Broadcast Notifications
description: ARCHETYPE: realtime_app. Shape: backend-only WebSocket server (no pages, no CRUD scaffold, no auth). Subscribe + broadcast are the CORE — not a POST endpoint wrapper. DO NOT build HTTP endpoints for messages. DO NOT build pages. DO NOT use requires login. The ENTIRE app is one `subscribe to 'channel':` block that listens for incoming messages on the 'notifications' channel and broadcasts them to all connected clients. Persist each broadcast to a Messages table for history. Include ONE HTTP endpoint GET /api/history that returns all Messages (tests need this to verify broadcasts landed). No other endpoints.
skeleton:
build for javascript backend

create a Messages table:
  text, required
  created_at_date, auto

# Your subscribe/broadcast handler here — use `subscribe to 'notifications':` and `broadcast to all message`
# Plus ONE GET /api/history endpoint for test verification
